content: project-memory auto-exports facts.jsonl on shutdown and auto-imports on session_start if jsonl is newer than db — enables cross-machine git sync
project-memory context injection: semantic subset (core sections + working memory + top-k semantic hits) when embeddings available and >20 facts; otherwise full dump

project-memory pressure system: onset→warning→auto-compact thresholds (config.pressureOnsetPercent/compactionWarningPercent/compactionAutoPercent). See extensions/project-memory/index.ts computeDegeneracyPressure()
project-memory memory is injected via before_agent_start event — only on firstTurn or postCompaction, not every turn
project-memory uses Ollama (qwen3-embedding) for semantic retrieval; falls back to FTS5 keyword search if unavailable. See extensions/project-memory/embeddings.ts
project-memory episodic memory: session narratives generated at shutdown via generateEpisode subagent, stored with embeddings for semantic search
pi-kit is a pi-package (extensions, prompts, skills, themes) for the pi coding agent. Root: ~/workspace/ai/pi-kit, package.json has `"keywords": ["pi-package"]`.

pi-kit extensions: 00-secrets, chronos, cleave, defaults.ts, distill.ts, render, local-inference, mcp-bridge, model-budget.ts, offline-driver.ts, project-memory, session-log.ts, shared-state.ts, spinner-verbs.ts, status-bar.ts, style.ts, terminal-title.ts, view, web-search, whoami.ts

project-memory extension: SQLite+WAL storage (.pi/memory/facts.db), semantic retrieval via Ollama qwen3-embedding, working memory buffer (25-slot cap), episodic session narratives, background extraction via subagent, global knowledge base (~/.pi/memory/global.db), JSONL export/import for git sync, context pressure system with auto-compaction.

project-memory tools (all 11): memory_query, memory_recall, memory_store, memory_supersede, memory_archive, memory_search_archive, memory_connect, memory_compact, memory_episodes, memory_focus, memory_release. See extensions/project-memory/index.ts

project-memory background extraction: runs after tool_execution_end via shouldExtract() triggers; killed on session_shutdown to prevent terminal corruption. See extensions/project-memory/triggers.ts + extraction-v2.ts
Tests run via `npx tsx --test extensions/**/*.test.ts`. No build step — extensions are TypeScript loaded directly.
project-memory status bar shows '0 facts · semantic T0' at session start — memory injection only fires on firstTurn/postCompaction, not at startup UI render time
project-memory linked minds incomplete — marked as "being rebuilt for SQLite store". /memory link command returns warning.
project-memory /memory edit opens editor but can't save changes back — shows "Direct editing not yet supported for SQLite store"
pi-kit skills: git, python, rust, style (in skills/ dir). Also external skills loaded via ~/.pi/agent/git/ symlinks (cleave, distill, session-log, visualize).

title: Seeded Project Memory with pi-kit Architecture Facts
narrative: The goal was to populate pi-kit's project-memory extension with foundational facts so future sessions start with context pre-loaded. Background extraction had already auto-captured 14 facts from the conversation; additional facts were manually stored covering known issues (linked minds incomplete, /memory edit broken), patterns (skills inventory), and constraints (test invocation). Four redundant overlapping facts were archived to reduce noise. The memory store now holds 18 facts across Architecture, Known Issues, Patterns & Conventions, and Constraints sections, ready for injection on the next session's first turn.
